Setting Team Properties

On Xbox Live, both teams and team members can have unique properties. Team properties include the team name, description, motto, and home page URL. Your game sets a team's data by calling XOnlineTeamSetProperties, passing it the team information in an XONLINE_TEAM_PROPERTIES structure.

In addition to the basic team information, your game can set title-specific team data. The XONLINE_TEAM_PROPERTIES structure contains an array of bytes for team data that can be formatted in any way you choose. The maximum array size is specified by the constant XONLINE_MAX_TEAM_DATA_SIZE.

Note    Your title should not allow players to change or delete a team name on a leaderboard. If a team name is changed or deleted, a blank name appears on the leaderboard for that team.

All team data must conform to Xbox Live standards. For example, it must not contain obscene or inappropriate language. The XOnlineTeamSetProperties function checks the contents of the XONLINE_TEAM_PROPERTIES structure for words that are not allowed on Xbox Live. However, it does not check the contents of the XONLINE_TEAM_PROPERTIES structure's TeamData array. Your title must filter out any content in this array that is not allowed. If the array contains strings, you can check them for objectionable content with the XOnlineStringVerify function.
